user_query:
I'm William Elwood Murray's grandniece. Please turn the provided skeleton under src/prototype into a small, local prototype that gathers a few public webpages mentioning him and renders a simple HTML page for my family.

Required deliverables and constraints:
1) Configure queries
- Edit src/prototype/config.yaml and set queries to include at least two distinct search queries that reflect my interests, for example variants of “William Elwood Murray” with terms like “general semantics”, “communication”, or “University of Colorado”. Keep per_query_limit as 5 and output_dir as output.

2) Implement and run the prototype
- Modify src/prototype/search_stub.py to implement the TODOs so that running:
  python src/prototype/search_stub.py
  will do the following:
  a) Perform live search engine queries from the Internet for each query in config.yaml using any public search engine accessible from a desktop computer (no paid APIs). Collect up to per_query_limit unique result URLs per query; record the exact query string alongside each URL.
  b) Fetch each result page’s HTML and extract:
     - page_title: the <title> text
     - meta_description: the value of <meta name="description"> or <meta property="og:description"> if present
  c) Use input/family_notes.json to load keyword filters. Retain only entries where page_title or meta_description contains at least one keyword (case-insensitive) from that file.
  d) Deduplicate across all queries by URL (keep the first occurrence).
  e) Write output/search_results.json as an array of objects with exactly these fields for each retained entry:
     - query (string)
     - url (string)
     - page_title (string or null)
     - meta_description (string or null)
     - source_domain (host part of the URL)
     - matched_keywords (array of strings that matched from input/family_notes.json)
     - fetched_at (ISO 8601 UTC timestamp)
  f) Render output/index.html by loading src/prototype/template.html and replacing:
     - {{TOTAL}} with the number of retained entries
     - {{GENERATED_AT}} with the current ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
     - {{ITEMS}} with list item markup for each retained entry, sorted by source_domain then page_title. Each item should include a clickable link to the URL whose link text is the page_title (or the URL if title is missing), and show the source_domain and matched_keywords in a small note.

3) General requirements
- All generated files must be written under the output directory specified in config.yaml and overwrite any existing files on re-run.
- Do not hardcode a single provider’s page structure; extract only generic HTML title and description fields as specified.
- It’s fine if fewer than per_query_limit entries are ultimately retained after filtering, but the pipeline should work end-to-end and produce both output/search_results.json and output/index.html.

Inputs you must use:
- input/family_notes.json: load keywords from here to filter and to populate matched_keywords.
- src/prototype/config.yaml: define queries and settings here and use them in your code.
- src/prototype/template.html: use this to render the final HTML prototype.

Outputs to produce:
- output/search_results.json (as specified schema)
- output/index.html (rendered from the template with placeholders replaced)

I’ll verify by inspecting the outputs against the schema, that queries were set in config.yaml, that keywords from input/family_notes.json were applied for filtering, and that the HTML was rendered from the template. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
